title: T-071 — Docs: ADR-002 + ADR-008 + ADR-028 + ADR-029 cross-reference notes for 03-TECHNICAL
prompt:
# T-071 — Docs: ADR-002 + ADR-008 + ADR-028 + ADR-029 cross-reference notes for 03-TECHNICAL

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §16
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
ADR cross-reference notes mapping each ADR to its task implementations.

Useful for reviewers + closeout traceability. No code changes — pure docs.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- design/specs/03-TECHNICAL-ADR-notes.md [create]

## Out of scope
- Editing the ADRs themselves

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the note exists, when reviewed, then 4 ADRs are cross-referenced with task IDs.
2. Given a reviewer reads it, when tracing closeout, then they can map ADR-029 cascade rule to T-024 directly.

## Test strategy
- Manual review.
- No automated tests.

## Risk red lines
- Do not edit ADR source files.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
